name: Delete a page for real (file gone, nav cleaned, others preserved)
prompt: Please delete the Services page from my site completely - I don't offer those services anymore. Take it out of the menu as well, but leave everything else exactly as it is.

setup.content:
---
title: "About"
description: "About our studio"
pageLayout: "default"
---
<Hero
  heading="About Us"
  subheading="We are a small family studio."
  align="center"
/>

---
title: "Services"
description: "What we offer"
pageLayout: "default"
---
<Hero
  heading="Our Services"
  subheading="Consulting, design, and delivery."
  align="center"
/>

site:
  name: "My Business"
  tagline: "The best in the business"
  primaryColor: "#4f46e5"
  bgEffect: ["grid", "aura", "hover"]
  logo: ""

contact:
  email: "hello@example.com"
  phone: ""

navigation:
  - label: "Home"
    url: "/"
  - label: "About"
    url: "/about"
  - label: "Services"
    url: "/services"

checks.judge: The Services page file was genuinely deleted - content/pages/services.mdx no longer exists (not merely emptied, stubbed, or unlinked) - and the 'Services' navigation entry was removed while the Home and About entries and pages stayed unchanged. Sam's reply truthfully reflects a real removal: it must NOT warn that an empty page may linger at the old address, and must NOT claim it cannot delete pages.

dry_run.text: I've deleted the Services page completely and removed it from your menu - Home and About are untouched. Say "publish" whenever you'd like this to go live.

edits.content/settings.yaml:
site:
  name: "My Business"
  tagline: "The best in the business"
  primaryColor: "#4f46e5"
  bgEffect: ["grid", "aura", "hover"]
  logo: ""

contact:
  email: "hello@example.com"
  phone: ""

navigation:
  - label: "Home"
    url: "/"
  - label: "About"
    url: "/about"
